description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic. This is a very long vertical strip from the middle of the letter. It was partially cut up, and the beginning of the letter was apparently rotated 180 degrees and glued back on. (Verso was then reused for Exodus 29:45–46; Malachi 3:4; Psalms 147:5–10; 95:7–11.) Mentions the sender's arrival in some place; "our elders"; "the West"; poverty; some sort of communal conflict; and "the heads of the Qaraites." Several lines toward the bottom are crossed and/or rubbed out. (Information in part from Goitein's index card and CUDL)
